_comment: Smoke fixture: All 3 deep workers agree — escalated (suspicious activity confirmed by all hypotheses). Expected aggregation: Rule 1 fires. action=escalated, confidence=max(0.82,0.79,0.71)=0.82.

finding.evidence: Login from IP 203.0.113.99 (TOR exit node, CN), actor not in baseline, 11 attempts in 4 minutes.

partial_transcript_summary: Parent investigate: called check-baseline (actor absent), search-events (11 events in 4 min burst), search-findings (2 related findings in 7 days). Confidence 0.48 — fell below 0.55 threshold. Fan-out triggered.

verdict.reason: Cannot confirm benign. Actor absent from baseline — no documented workflow, no upstream trigger, no consistent source IP. Provenance chain cold. Credential theft indistinguishable from legitimate use with available evidence.

evidence_chain: baseline-stats: no time-of-day pattern for this actor in any source
search-events: 11 login-attempt events in 4-minute window from 203.0.113.99
read-config: github connector logs last 90 days — no gap that would explain missing baseline
Positive confirming evidence of legitimacy: NONE FOUND

verdict.reason: Malicious hypothesis confirmed. Attack vector: credential stuffing from TOR exit node. 11 attempts in 4 minutes consistent with automated tooling. Actor absent from baseline eliminates insider-legitimacy explanation. 2 related findings in 7 days — coordinated campaign pattern.

evidence_chain: check-baseline: actor not found — eliminates known-user benign path
search-events: burst pattern (11 attempts / 4 min) consistent with automated credential stuffing
search-findings: 2 related findings involving 203.0.113.99 in prior 7 days — campaign indicator
IP 203.0.113.99 maps to TOR exit node (CN geolocation) — anonymization consistent with attack
No privilege change detected — attack appears to be access-gain phase only

verdict.reason: Investigation confirms data is sufficient for escalation. IP reputation data (TOR exit classification) and burst pattern are unambiguous. Missing: whether any login succeeded. Even without success confirmation, the attack pattern warrants escalation.

evidence_chain: read-config: github connector captures login-attempt events including success/failure field
search-events: all 11 events show status=failed — no successful authentication
Observable that would flip verdict: if any event showed status=success, would escalate with higher urgency
Data gap: no MFA log to confirm whether MFA was challenged — this gap does not change escalation recommendation
Conclusion: data is sufficient for escalation decision; gap is non-determinative
